title: psoralea pilifera
intro: Psoralea pilifera, also known as Wolf's Bane or Psoralea, is a perennial herb native to the eastern United States and parts of Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Psoralea pilifera include improving skin conditions such as eczema and acne, reducing inflammation and pain, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the root and leaves of the plant are typically dried and made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
